items.title: Interstate fans say keep Gather Round in Adelaide as AFL boss probed on why AFLW not part of the program

children.content: Crows AFLW player Ebony Marinoff has questioned the AFL's CEO on why the women's program hasn't been part of the successful Gather Round in Adelaide.

children.content: Zak Butters defends himself after the Power star was reported for using inappropriate language towards an umpire.

articles.title: Fans living it up in Adelaide for Gather Round but future of event uncertain

children.content: Occupancy rates and food and drink sales are up on last year's Gather Round, says the Australian Hotels Association, despite concerns fuel prices would deter interstate visitors.

items.title: Hiker found 'safe and well' after spending 24 hours lost in Victoria's north-west

children.content: Police have located a hiker who separated from his group and was missing for more than 24 hours in Victoria's north-west.

image.altText: Police finds missing camper Andy

items.title: Independent supermarkets struggle to keep prices down as Iran war hurts

children.content: Suppliers are charging more to deliver groceries to supermarkets as fuel prices increase, putting pressure on smaller retailers.

image.altText: Fresh vegetables on three black shelves with price stickers in front of the items.

items.title: From No Pokies to no MPs: Last SA Best member proud of party's legacy after losing seat

children.content: SA Best, the party formed by stunt-loving politician Nick Xenophon, has closed its chapter in South Australian political history with its final member Connie Bonaros losing her seat in last month's state election.

items.title: 'Epic in scale': APY Lands exhibition opens at NGA after three-year delay

children.content: An exhibition of First Nations art from the APY Lands in South Australia brings together 30 large-scale works by nearly 50 artists.

image.altText: Two woman sitting on while painting.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

image.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: Bricks and brutalism being added to heritage list, as owners raise concerns

children.content: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

items.title: The fourth-year med student from Broken Hill who won the Stawell Gift

children.content: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: Olufemi Komolafe holds the trophy after winning the 2026 Men's Stawell Gift

items.title: SA GP with an 'unhealthy obsession' for laser therapy disqualified

children.content: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

image.altText: Close-up photo of a silver and white stethoscope. Dec 2022.

items.title: Commuters trapped for hours in freeway gridlock blast lack of signage

children.content: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

image.altText: Cars fill a freeway

items.title: Canada has flipped the script on childcare. Could Australia copy?

children.content: Australia definitely won't nationalise childcare, even though the data suggests there's a strong case. Perhaps a more feasible idea would be to copy what Canada has done.

image.altText: A child walks at childcare.

items.title: Fuel crisis sees some some delaying retirement and working from home

children.content: The COVID-19 pandemic brought job losses, working from home and economic instability. Some fear the US-Israel war on Iran, which has seen fuel prices spike, has created a similar struggles.

image.altText: A woman sitting with a guitar looking away from the camera.

items.title: 'Like a flea on an elephant's back': The unexpected rise of solar power

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

images.altText: Jason Day grimaces at the Masters

items.title: 'Missed opportunity': Astronomers lament loss of telescope partnership

items.title: Five takeaways from US-Iran peace talks

children.content: After an absolute marathon of overnight negotiations and historic high-level talks between the United States and Iran, a deal to end the war that has thrown the Middle East and global energy supplies into chaos has not been reached.

items.title: Secret documents reveal landmark housing project's budget headache

items.title: Loved, but costly — is the end near for this heritage railway?

children.content: The West Coast Wilderness Railway in Tasmania is a beloved attraction and an important employer. The state government says it wants the railway to have a sustainable future, but hasn't said how that could be achieved.

image.altText: Locomotive travels through a forest railway track.

items.title: Queensland's north set for soaking as ex-cyclone Maila brings heavy rains

children.content: Beautiful one day, heavy rain, dangerous surf and frosts, the next — Queensland faces a mixed bag of weather throughout the state over the coming days.

items.title: Caretaker files for possession of impressionist Frederick McCubbin's former country home

items.title: Love story: 'It's not my kidney now, it's yours. I'm just holding it for you'

children.content: Despite a 10-year age gap and geographical separation, Sajarn and Codey Stow have retained a close bond since childhood. That bond has only strengthened since Codey received a donor kidney from his brother last November.

items.title: No work, no savings, no way to wait: India's migrant workers head home

children.content: The war in the Middle East has brought one of India's largest industrial hubs to a standstill. In the city of Morbi, hundreds of thousands of workers have lost their jobs, after gas supplies were disrupted thousands of kilometres away.

children.content: The singer recorded over 12,000 songs and was one of Indian cinema's most celebrated voices.
